review_guidelines.title: Migration Files Must Follow Strict Naming Convention
review_guidelines.body: Ensure database migration files follow the required timestamp-based naming pattern to maintain chronological order and enable proper migration tracking Migration files in core/server/data/migrations/versions/ (excluding versions 1.x, 2.x, 3.x) match the pattern: YYYY-MM-DD-HH-MM-descriptive-name-with-hyphens (e.g., 2022-04-05-12-00-add-member-alert-setting.js) Migration files do not follow the required timestamp format or use underscores, camelCase, or other naming styles

review_guidelines.title: Enforce Module Boundary Between core/shared and core/server
review_guidelines.body: Maintain architectural separation by preventing core/shared modules from depending on core/server code, ensuring shared code remains truly reusable across server and frontend Files in core/shared/ do not contain require() or import statements that reference core/server/ paths Files in core/shared/ import or require modules from core/server/

review_guidelines.title: Enforce Module Boundary Between core/shared and core/frontend
review_guidelines.body: Maintain architectural separation by preventing core/shared modules from depending on core/frontend code, ensuring shared code remains truly reusable across server and frontend Files in core/shared/ do not contain require() or import statements that reference core/frontend/ paths Files in core/shared/ import or require modules from core/frontend/

review_guidelines.title: Database Schema Must Not Include User Audit Fields
review_guidelines.body: Prevent using created_by and updated_by fields in database schema to enforce the use of the action log for tracking user actions, providing better audit trails and separation of concerns The schema definition in core/server/data/schema/schema.js does not contain properties named 'created_by' or 'updated_by' The schema definition includes 'created_by' or 'updated_by' fields

review_guidelines.title: Database Migrations Must Not Use Loop Constructs
review_guidelines.body: Prevent performance issues in database migrations by prohibiting loop constructs (for, while, forEach) that can cause migrations to run slowly or fail on large datasets Migration files in core/server/data/migrations/versions/ do not contain for statements, for-of statements, for-in statements, while statements, forEach calls, or _.each calls Migration files contain any loop constructs including for, for-of, for-in, while, forEach, or _.each

review_guidelines.title: Database Migrations Must Not Use Multiple JOIN Statements
review_guidelines.body: Prevent complex and poorly performing database queries in migrations by prohibiting multiple JOIN operations in a single Knex query block Migration files do not chain multiple join/innerJoin/leftJoin calls on a single Knex query object Migration files contain Knex queries with multiple chained join operations

review_guidelines.title: Database Migrations Must Not Return Early from Loops
review_guidelines.body: Prevent data processing errors in migrations by ensuring all records are processed when loops are used (in legacy migrations or utility functions) Migration files do not contain return statements inside loop bodies Migration files contain return statements within loop constructs

review_guidelines.title: API Endpoint Controllers Must Have Type Annotations
review_guidelines.body: Ensure type safety and IDE support for API endpoint controllers by requiring JSDoc type annotations from @tryghost/api-framework API endpoint controller objects in core/server/api/endpoints/ include JSDoc type comment '@type {import('@tryghost/api-framework').Controller}' API endpoint controllers lack the required JSDoc type annotation

review_guidelines.title: API Endpoints Must Enforce Complexity Limits
review_guidelines.body: Maintain code quality and prevent overly complex API endpoint implementations by enforcing complexity metrics on endpoint handler functions API endpoint files in core/server/api/endpoints/ pass the 'ghost/ghost-custom/max-api-complexity' ESLint rule API endpoint functions exceed the maximum allowed cyclomatic complexity threshold

review_guidelines.title: Internationalization Files Must Use Kebab-Case Naming
review_guidelines.body: Ensure consistent file naming across all i18n translation files to maintain a uniform codebase structure and simplify automated tooling All files in ghost/i18n/ use kebab-case naming (lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and dots only): matching pattern ^[a-z0-9.-]+$ i18n files use camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, or contain uppercase letters

review_guidelines.title: Admin React Apps Must Use Kebab-Case File Naming
review_guidelines.body: Enforce consistent kebab-case file naming convention across React admin applications to align with the codebase migration to kebab-case naming standards All files in apps/admin-x-* directories use kebab-case naming (lowercase letters, numbers, hyphens, and dots only): matching pattern ^[a-z0-9.-]+$ Admin app files use camelCase, PascalCase, snake_case, or contain uppercase letters

review_guidelines.title: React Components Must Sort JSX Props in Standard Order
review_guidelines.body: Maintain consistent and readable JSX code by enforcing a standard ordering of component props: reserved props first, callbacks last, shorthand props last JSX elements in React files have props ordered according to: reserved props (key, ref) first, then regular props alphabetically, then callbacks, then shorthand props last JSX elements have props in non-standard order (e.g., callbacks before regular props, shorthand before callbacks)
